title: Getting started with n8n
text: Getting started with n8n 2026-04-01 n8n is a workflow automation tool with a node-based editor. Key ideas: - **Triggers** start a flow (schedule, webhook, manual). - **Nodes** connect data from one step to the next. - Expressions use `{{ $json.field }}` to map prior outputs. ```javascript // Example: map items in a Code node return items.map((item) => ({ json: { ...item.json, processed: true }, })); ``` - Installed n8n locally with Docker. - Built a two-node flow: **Webhook → Respond to Webhook** for a test endpoint. First run failed because port `5678` was already in use by another app. Changed the host port mapping in `docker run` to `-p 15678:5678` and opened `http://localhost:15678`. Add a third node that calls an external API and logs the response. https://github.com/yourusername/n8n-learning-journal Optional **body** markdown below front matter appears under a **Notes** section if you add content here.

title: Webhooks and APIs
text: Webhooks and APIs 2026-04-02 - HTTP webhooks push events to a URL you control; APIs are pull-based unless streaming. - Verify signatures (e.g. HMAC) when consuming third-party webhooks. - Idempotency keys help avoid duplicate side effects on retries. - Extended the Day 1 flow with an **HTTP Request** node hitting a JSON placeholder API. - Stored responses in a simple Google Sheet via a manual export (no OAuth yet). Occasional `429 Too Many Requests` when testing too fast against the free API. Added a **Wait** node (2s) and reduced loop iterations during development. Experiment with authenticated APIs and environment variables in n8n.

title: Claude automation experiments
text: Claude automation experiments 2026-04-03 Using an LLM inside an automation means you must design for: - **Structured outputs** (JSON schema or clear delimiters). - **Retries** when parsing fails. - **Cost and latency** budgets per run. ```json { "role": "user", "content": "Summarize the following log line in one sentence." } ``` - Prototype script that reads a text file, sends chunks to the API, and writes summaries to `out/summary.md`. - Wrapped the call in a small CLI with readline prompts for the file path. Model occasionally returned markdown fences around JSON, breaking `JSON.parse`. Stripped code fences with a regex before parse, and retried once with a stricter system prompt. Move prompts to external templates and add unit tests for the JSON extractor. https://github.com/yourusername/claude-automation-sandbox
